title: python.lang.security.audit.subprocess-shell-true.subprocess-shell-true
description: Found 'subprocess' function 'run' with 'shell=True'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use 'shell=False' instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
owasp_categories: A01:2017 - Injection, A03:2021 - Injection, A05:2025 - Injection

title: python.lang.security.audit.eval-detected.eval-detected
description: Detected the use of eval(). eval() can be dangerous if used to evaluate dynamic content. If this content can be input from outside the program, this may be a code injection vulnerability. Ensure evaluated content is not definable by external sources.

owasp_categories: A03:2021 - Injection, A05:2025 - Injection

title: python.lang.security.deserialization.pickle.avoid-pickle

title: python.sqlalchemy.security.sqlalchemy-execute-raw-query.sqlalchemy-execute-raw-query
description: Avoiding SQL string concatenation: untrusted input concatenated with raw SQL query can result in SQL Injection. In order to execute raw query safely, prepared statement should be used. SQLAlchemy provides TextualSQL to easily used prepared statement with named parameters. For complex SQL composition, use SQL Expression Language or Schema Definition Language. In most cases, SQLAlchemy ORM will be a better option.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
owasp_categories: A01:2017 - Injection, A03:2021 - Injection, A05:2025 - Injection
code_excerpt: cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='{username}' AND password='{password}';")

title: python.django.security.injection.ssrf.ssrf-injection-requests.ssrf-injection-requests
description: Data from request object is passed to a new server-side request. This could lead to a server-side request forgery (SSRF). To mitigate, ensure that schemes and hosts are validated against an allowlist, do not forward the response to the user, and ensure proper authentication and transport-layer security in the proxied request. See https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/Server_Side_Request_Forgery to learn more about SSRF vulnerabilities.
owasp_categories: A10:2021 - Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF), A01:2025 - Broken Access Control

title: python.django.security.injection.raw-html-format.raw-html-format
description: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`django.shortcuts.render`) which will safely render HTML instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
owasp_categories: A07:2017 - Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), A03:2021 - Injection, A05:2025 - Injection
code_excerpt: message = f"Hello, {name}! Welcome to our website."

title: python.flask.security.injection.raw-html-concat.raw-html-format
description: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`flask.render_template`) which will safely render HTML instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
owasp_categories: A07:2017 - Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), A03:2021 - Injection, A05:2025 - Injection

title: python.flask.security.audit.xss.make-response-with-unknown-content.make-response-with-unknown-content
description: Be careful with `flask.make_response()`. If this response is rendered onto a webpage, this could create a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability. `flask.make_response()` will not autoescape HTML. If you are rendering HTML, write your HTML in a template file and use `flask.render_template()` which will take care of escaping. If you are returning data from an API, consider using `flask.jsonify()`.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
owasp_categories: A07:2017 - Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), A03:2021 - Injection, A05:2025 - Injection

cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
owasp_categories: A05:2017 - Broken Access Control, A01:2021 - Broken Access Control

title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.best-practice.unspecified-open-encoding
description: Missing 'encoding' parameter. 'open()' uses device locale encodings by default, corrupting files with special characters. Specify the encoding to ensure cross-platform support when opening files in text mode (e.g. encoding="utf-8").

owasp_categories: A05:2021 - Security Misconfiguration

title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled

title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.
owasp_categories: A01:2021 - Broken Access Control

title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.injection.tainted-sql-string
description: Detected user input used to manually construct a SQL string. This is usually bad practice because manual construction could accidentally result in a SQL injection. An attacker could use a SQL injection to steal or modify contents of the database. Instead, use a parameterized query which is available by default in most database engines. Alternatively, consider using an object-relational mapper (ORM) such as Sequelize which will protect your queries.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
owasp_categories: A01:2017 - Injection, A03:2021 - Injection
code_excerpt: db.query(`SELECT * FROM products WHERE productName LIKE '%${name}%'`, (err, rows, fields) => {

title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.formatted-sql-query
description: Detected possible formatted SQL query. Use parameterized queries instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
owasp_categories: A01:2017 - Injection, A03:2021 - Injection
code_excerpt: cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='{username}' AND password='{password}';")

title: app.opengrep-rules.python.sqlalchemy.security.sqlalchemy-execute-raw-query
description: Avoiding SQL string concatenation: untrusted input concatenated with raw SQL query can result in SQL Injection. In order to execute raw query safely, prepared statement should be used. SQLAlchemy provides TextualSQL to easily used prepared statement with named parameters. For complex SQL composition, use SQL Expression Language or Schema Definition Language. In most cases, SQLAlchemy ORM will be a better option.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
owasp_categories: A01:2017 - Injection, A03:2021 - Injection
code_excerpt: cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='{username}' AND password='{password}';")

title: app.opengrep-rules.python.django.security.injection.ssrf.ssrf-injection-requests
description: Data from request object is passed to a new server-side request. This could lead to a server-side request forgery (SSRF). To mitigate, ensure that schemes and hosts are validated against an allowlist, do not forward the response to the user, and ensure proper authentication and transport-layer security in the proxied request. See https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/Server_Side_Request_Forgery to learn more about SSRF vulnerabilities.
owasp_categories: A10:2021 - Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.injection.ssrf-requests
description: Data from request object is passed to a new server-side request. This could lead to a server-side request forgery (SSRF). title: app.opengrep-rules.python.requests.best-practice.use-timeout
description: Detected a 'requests' call without a timeout set. By default, 'requests' calls wait until the connection is closed. This means a 'requests' call without a timeout will hang the program if a response is never received. Consider setting a timeout for all 'requests'.

title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.
owasp_categories: A01:2021 - Broken Access Control

title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.unsafe-formatstring
description: Detected string concatenation with a non-literal variable in a util.format / console.log function. If an attacker injects a format specifier in the string, it will forge the log message. Try to use constant values for the format string.
cwe_ids: CWE-134: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String
owasp_categories: A01:2021 - Broken Access Control

title: app.opengrep-rules.python.django.security.injection.raw-html-format
description: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`django.shortcuts.render`) which will safely render HTML instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
owasp_categories: A07:2017 - Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), A03:2021 - Injection
code_excerpt: message = f"Hello, {name}! Welcome to our website."

title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.injection.raw-html-format
description: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`flask.render_template`) which will safely render HTML instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
owasp_categories: A07:2017 - Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), A03:2021 - Injection
code_excerpt: message = f"Hello, {name}! Welcome to our website."

title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.xss.make-response-with-unknown-content
description: Be careful with `flask.make_response()`. If this response is rendered onto a webpage, this could create a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability. `flask.make_response()` will not autoescape HTML. If you are rendering HTML, write your HTML in a template file and use `flask.render_template()` which will take care of escaping. If you are returning data from an API, consider using `flask.jsonify()`.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
owasp_categories: A07:2017 - Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), A03:2021 - Injection

title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml
description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
owasp_categories: A04:2017 - XML External Entities (XXE), A05:2021 - Security Misconfiguration

title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml-parse
description: The native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks.  These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service. Do not use this library to parse untrusted input. Instead  the Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml`.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
owasp_categories: A04:2017 - XML External Entities (XXE), A05:2021 - Security Misconfiguration
